rationale: To deter Tethys from exploiting our border vulnerabilities by demonstrating unwavering readiness for escalation.
To secure critical energy infrastructure near the border and prevent sabotage or infiltration.
A targeted strike could disrupt Tethysian logistics if intelligence confirms imminent hostile maneuvers.
Conduct covert sabotage on Tethysian energy transit infrastructure to pressure their economic leverage over Novaris.
Provide select allies with evidence of Tethysian aggression to build diplomatic and intelligence alignment.
Stage a deniable incident to discredit Tethys in the information space and justify our military posture.
Secure reserves of critical energy supplies to insulate the domestic economy from Tethysian pipeline disruptions.
Stabilize the national currency against anticipated sanctions-driven volatility through controlled market interventions.
Accelerate talks with Meridian to reroute 15% of energy exports and reduce dependency on Tethysian transit.
A precision strike on forward Novaris logistical hubs will exploit their overextension and degrade operational readiness without triggering full escalation.
Conducting large-scale armored exercises near the border demonstrates resolve and prepares our forces for rapid mobilization if needed.
Disrupting Novaris military communications networks creates battlefield confusion and offsets their numerical advantage.
Covertly targeting key pipeline control systems in occupied zones will amplify Novaris’s energy export vulnerabilities and strain domestic stability.
Orchestrating a deniable false-flag incident can undermine Novaris’s international legitimacy and fracture diplomatic support.
Deploying deep-penetration surveillance assets will provide early warning of offensive intentions and identify new asymmetric opportunities.
Rallying regional partners strengthens our diplomatic front and increases the cost of Novaris aggression through collective pressure.
Establishing corridors enhances our moral standing, draws contrast with Novaris’s militarism, and opens channels for intelligence flow.
Quietly exploring off-ramps preserves crisis stability and may expose fractures within Novaris’s leadership.
Aggressively stockpiling wheat and energy insulates us from supply shocks and buys time for military and diplomatic efforts to take effect.
Imposing targeted export bans on dual-use technologies squeezes Novaris’s war economy without provoking immediate retaliation.
Coordinating with allies to weaken Novaris’s currency erodes their purchasing power and amplifies existing economic strain.
